steps.title: Separate the planet types
steps.content: On rocky planets, atmospheric NH<sub>3</sub> likely cannot reach detectable levels without biological activity. On mini-Neptunes, pressure and temperature alone can produce atmospheric NH<sub>3</sub>, so its presence there would not point specifically to life.

steps.title: Apply the condition
steps.content: A turns the evidence into a claim about identifying planet type, which the text does not make. B treats absence of NH3 as proof about life, which is unsupported. D says NH3 is unreliable in both planet types, contradicting the rocky-planet finding. The valid statement must treat the same gas differently depending on whether biology is needed to explain it. The answer is C.
